title: Uniform Continuity
statement: Let $A \subseteq \mathbb{R}$ and let $f:A \to \mathbb{R}$. The function $f$ is uniformly continuous on $A$ if and only if for every $\varepsilon > 0$ there exists $\delta > 0$ such that for all $x,u \in A$ the implication $|x-u| < \delta \Rightarrow |f(x)-f(u)| < \varepsilon$ holds.
